---
title: "Soil Organic Carbon Model Intercomparison"
subtitle: "Bayesian Calibration Framework for Finnish Forests"
author: "Lorenzo"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  beamer_presentation:
    theme: "Madrid"
    colortheme: "default"
    fig_caption: false
    includes:
      in_header: preamble.tex
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
# rmarkdown::render("soc_intercomparison_slides.Rmd")
```

# Current State: Multi-Model Framework

The models are so far **implemented in R** for faster prototyping. The code seems fast enough to run it in R via Bayesiantools, but multi-threading on Roihu needs still to be tested. Re-coding possible but prefer to avoid.  

**Five SOC Models Implemented & Debugged:**

- **Yasso family**: Yasso07, Yasso15, Yasso20 (chemical fractionation)
- **RothC**: Decomposability-based pools
- **Q-model**: Continuous quality theory

\vspace{0.3cm}

**Implementation:**

- One layer of model-specific input rerouting for compatibility
- Analytical steady-state solutions for Yasso07, Yasso15, Yasso20 and RothC
- Optimized Q-model (Swedish hybrid init + Rcpp)

---

\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
    node distance=0.7cm,
    box/.style={rectangle, draw, minimum width=2.2cm, minimum height=0.7cm, align=center, font=\small},
    bigbox/.style={rectangle, draw, thick, minimum width=2.2cm, minimum height=0.8cm, align=center, font=\small},
    arrow/.style={->, >=stealth, thick}
]
% Input data - using normal boxes
\node[box, fill=blue!10] (climate) at (0,0) {Climate\\ T, precip};
\node[box, fill=blue!10] (litter) at (3,0) {Litter\\ AWEN};
\node[box, fill=blue!10] (soil) at (6,0) {Initial\\ SOC};

% Input matching layer
\node[bigbox, fill=green!10, minimum width=6cm] (matching) at (3,-1.2) {Input Matching Functions layer};

% Model wrappers - expanded Yasso versions
\node[bigbox, fill=yellow!15] (yasso07) at (-1.5,-2.5) {Yasso07};
\node[bigbox, fill=yellow!15] (yasso15) at (1,-2.5) {Yasso15};
\node[bigbox, fill=yellow!15] (yasso20) at (3.5,-2.5) {Yasso20};
\node[bigbox, fill=yellow!15] (rothc) at (6,-2.5) {RothC};
\node[bigbox, fill=yellow!15] (qmodel) at (8.5,-2.5) {Q-model};

% Key components
\node[bigbox, fill=orange!20, minimum width=4cm] (steady) at (0,-3.8) {Steady State};
\node[bigbox, fill=orange!20, minimum width=4cm] (forward) at (6,-3.8) {Forward\\ Simulation};

% Output
\node[box, fill=purple!10] (output) at (3.5,-5) {SOC\\ predictions};

% Arrows from inputs to matching
\draw[arrow] (climate) -- (matching);
\draw[arrow] (litter) -- (matching);
\draw[arrow] (soil) -- (matching);

% Arrows from matching to models
\draw[arrow] (matching) -- (yasso07);
\draw[arrow] (matching) -- (yasso15);
\draw[arrow] (matching) -- (yasso20);
\draw[arrow] (matching) -- (rothc);
\draw[arrow] (matching) -- (qmodel);

% Arrows from models to components
\draw[arrow] (yasso07) -- (steady);
\draw[arrow] (yasso15) -- (steady);
\draw[arrow] (yasso20) -- (steady);
\draw[arrow] (rothc) -- (steady);
\draw[arrow] (qmodel) -- (steady);

\draw[arrow] (yasso07) -- (forward);
\draw[arrow] (yasso15) -- (forward);
\draw[arrow] (yasso20) -- (forward);
\draw[arrow] (rothc) -- (forward);
\draw[arrow] (qmodel) -- (forward);

% Arrows from components to output
\draw[arrow] (steady) -- (output);
\draw[arrow] (forward) -- (output);
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
\vspace{0.3cm}
---


# Key Optimizations: Q-Model

**Challenge:** Q-model steady state is a coarse approximation and causes differences in the first decade or two C balance. The initial cohort matrix needs to be initialized with numerical spin up, but this is computationally intensive.  

\vspace{0.5cm}

**Solution 1: Swedish Hybrid Initialization**

- Combines analytical steady-state with numerical adjustment
- Avoids full numerical spinup from zero
- \textbf{Result:} 10--20x speedup while maintaining accuracy

\vspace{0.5cm}

**Solution 2: Rcpp Compilation**

- Performance-critical components compiled to C++
- Maintains R interface for integration
- Essential for feasibility of full-scale calibration


---


# Example: Running the Multi-Model Wrapper
\footnotesize
```r
    results_default <- run_soc_models_multipplot_calibration(
      input_df = input_data,
      site_df = site_data,
      models = c("yasso07", "yasso15", "yasso20", "rothc", "q_model"),
      params_list = NULL,  # NULL means use defaults
      spinup_years = 100
    )
```
\normalsize
Parameter values can be passed to the wrapper:  

\footnotesize
```r
    results_custom <- run_soc_models_multipplot_calibration(
      input_df = input_data,
      site_df = site_data,
      models = "q_model",  # Only run Q-model
      params_list = list(q_model = custom_q_params),  # Custom params
      spinup_years = 100
    )
```


# Bayesian Calibration Framework

**Successful Pilot Runs:**

- BayesianTools package (DEzs sampler as default)
- Good convergence on small datasets
- Proper parameter passing validated
- Tested locally on Mac

\vspace{0.5cm}

**Calibration Strategy:**

- Models run in \textbf{independent batches}, not joint distribution
- Batches will be independent executions
- Per-plot calibration across full dataset

\vspace{0.5cm}

**Scaling Path:**
- Local testing → \textbf{Directly to Roihu cluster}
- Parallel processing across forest plots

---

# Future Sampler Implementation

\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
    node distance=0.7cm,
    box/.style={rectangle, draw, minimum width=2.5cm, minimum height=0.7cm, align=center, font=\small},
    bigbox/.style={rectangle, draw, thick, minimum width=3cm, minimum height=0.9cm, align=center, font=\small},
    arrow/.style={->, >=stealth, thick}
]

% Top: Data layer
\node[box, fill=blue!10] (obs) at (0,0) {Observed SOC};
\node[box, fill=blue!10] (drivers) at (3.5,0) {Environmental\\ Drivers};

% MCMC Sampler
\node[bigbox, fill=red!15] (sampler) at (1.75,-1.5) {\textbf{MCMC Sampler}\\ (DEzs - BayesianTools)};

% Stratification testing
\node[bigbox, fill=green!15] (strat) at (1.75,-3) {\textbf{Stratification Testing}};

\node[box, fill=orange!10] (nostrat) at (-0.5,-4.5) {No strata\\ (whole profile)};
\node[box, fill=orange!10] (onestrat) at (4,-4.5) {Best single\\ stratification};

% Model results
\node[bigbox, fill=purple!15] (results) at (1.75,-6) {\textbf{Model Comparison}\\ per Stratification};

% Arrows
\draw[arrow] (obs) -- (sampler);
\draw[arrow] (drivers) -- (sampler);

\draw[arrow] (sampler) -- (strat);

\draw[arrow] (strat) -- (nostrat);
\draw[arrow] (strat) -- (onestrat);

\draw[arrow] (nostrat) -- (results);
\draw[arrow] (onestrat) -- (results);

\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}

\vspace{0.2cm}
\footnotesize
\textbf{Strategy:} Test models as-is with different stratification approaches

\textbf{Goal:} Identify best stratification level (likely whole profile vs single horizon split)

---

# Planned Analysis Approach
Test each model (Yasso07, Yasso15, Yasso20, RothC, Q-model) with:

\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{No stratification}: Whole soil profile integration
\item \textbf{Single stratification}: Best horizon split to test
\end{itemize}

\vspace{0.3cm}

**Models tested as-is**

Models are kept as much as possible close to original formulations, and differences between them are therefore many and impossible to disentangle.

\vspace{0.3cm}

**Goals:** 

1. Identify whether stratification improves model fit across models
2. Ensemble uncertainty predictions
3. Compare different modeling approaches (although not very rigorously)

---

# Computational Strategy & Challenges

**Scaling Approach:**

- Local testing (Mac M-series) for validation on 3-4 plots
- Then deployment to Roihu cluster
- Parallel processing across all plots in independent model batches for risk mitigation

\vspace{0.5cm}

**Expected Challenges:**

\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{Runtime:} Full calibration across all plots × 5 models = at least \textbf{one day} of execution \textbf{per batch}
\item This with 2000 steps chains. Convergence might be off.
\item \textbf{Factorial design might be computationally unfeasible!!}
\end{itemize}


---


# Data requirements at plot level

**Forest time series (MONTHLY!):**

- AWEN chemical fractions 
- Distinct AWEN fractions for: Foliage, Branches, Fine Roots, Coarse Roots, Understorey
- Repeated SOC measurements across Finnish forests

\vspace{0.3cm}

**Forest time series (CONSTANT):**

- Species composition: Pine, Spruce, Birch (for Q)
- Clay (for RothC)

**Environmental Drivers  (MONTHLY!):**

- Monthly temperature and precipitation


---

# Data requirements

\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
    grow=right,
    level 1/.style={sibling distance=0.7cm, level distance=2cm},
    level 2/.style={sibling distance=0.28cm, level distance=1.6cm, font=\scriptsize},
    every node/.style={font=\tiny, align=center},
    edge from parent/.style={draw, -latex}
]

% Root node
\node[rectangle, draw, thick, fill=blue!10, minimum width=1.2cm, minimum height=0.6cm] {HIKET\\Input}
    child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=green!10, minimum width=1cm, minimum height=0.4cm] {Foliage}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.05cm] {A}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.05cm ] {W}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.05cm ] {E}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.05cm ] {N}}
    }
    child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=green!10, minimum width=1cm, minimum height=0.4cm] {Branches}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {A}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {W}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {E}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {N}}
    }
    child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=green!10, minimum width=1cm, minimum height=0.4cm] {Stems}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {A}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {W}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {E}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {N}}
    }
    child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=green!10, minimum width=1cm, minimum height=0.4cm] {Fine\\roots}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.2cm] {A}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.2cm] {W}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.2cm] {E}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.2cm] {N}}
    }
    child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=green!10, minimum width=1cm, minimum height=0.4cm] {Coarse\\roots}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {A}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {W}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {E}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {N}}
    }
    child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=green!10, minimum width=1cm, minimum height=0.4cm] {Under-\\storey}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {A}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {W}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {E}}
        child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=orange!20, minimum width=0.5cm, minimum height=0.25cm] {N}}
    }
    child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=cyan!10, minimum width=1cm, minimum height=0.4cm] {Temp}}
    child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=cyan!10, minimum width=1cm, minimum height=0.4cm] {Precip}}
    child {node[rectangle, draw, fill=purple!10, minimum width=1cm, minimum height=0.4cm] {SOC\\obs}};

\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}

---

# Climate Scaling Implementation Across Models

| Model | Time scale | Temperature Response | Moisture Response |
|-------|-------|---------------------|-------------------|
| Yasso07 | Annual |  Gaussian (mean, range) | Precipitation-based annual modifier |
| Yasso15 | Annual, pool specific |  Gaussian (mean, range) | Precipitation-based annual modifier |
| Yasso20 | Monthly, pool specific|  Gaussian (mean, range) | Precipitation-based annual modifier |
| RothC   | Montly |  Arrhenius  | Moisture deficit model (rain, evap, SMC) |
| Q-model | Annual |  Linear | Absent |

For Q a quality dependent scaled Arrhenius is available, but not implemented yet.


# Difficulties comparing the models

The models all rely on very different:

* Structures  
* Response functions  
* Input assumptions  
* Calibration assumptions  

Ideally we would test these **factorially** (as originally discussed), but I don't think HIKET scope allows for that...


# Open questions

* Stratification levels?  
* Is it fine to skip factorial model structure comparison?
* Discuss initialization and input unification approaches
* Validation? Crossvalidation or leaving a set out? I am more for the latter...



# Status & Next steps

**Status:**

- The model wrapper is ready for forward runs


\vspace{0.3cm}

**Next steps:**

1. Forward runs without calibration, comparison with measurements 
2. Base model comparison results for policy (mid-project deliverable)
3. Full-scale calibration against measured SOC run on cluster across all plots
4. Calibrated model comparison results for policy (final deliverable)

There might be hiccups on the way.
